params.text:
Near misses, incidents and RIDDOR

Reporting is how services learn and stop the next incident. A near miss is an event that could have caused harm but didn't — for example a hoist sling that slipped but was caught in time. Report near misses just as you would an actual accident: they are a free warning.

Record accidents and incidents in your organisation's system (often an accident book or online form) promptly and accurately, sticking to the facts.

RIDDOR

The Reporting of Injuries, Diseases and Dangerous Occurrences Regulations (RIDDOR) require certain serious work-related events to be reported to the HSE by the responsible person (usually your employer, not you personally). Reportable events include:

  • Work-related deaths and specified injuries (such as fractures other than to fingers/thumbs/toes, amputations, or serious burns).
  • Injuries that keep a worker off their normal duties for more than 7 days.
  • Certain occupational diseases and dangerous occurrences.

Deaths, specified injuries and dangerous occurrences must be reported without delay; the over-7-day injury report must be made within 15 days. Your job is to report promptly and accurately up the line so the responsible person can decide if RIDDOR applies.

params.question: A colleague slips on an unmarked wet floor, is taken to hospital and is signed off their normal duties for 10 days. What is true about reporting this?

answers.text: It is likely reportable to the HSE under RIDDOR as an over-7-day injury, and you should report it internally straight away.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. An over-7-day incapacity from a work accident is normally RIDDOR-reportable. Report it internally at once so your employer can notify the HSE within the time limit.

answers.text: There's nothing to report because it was just a slip and no bones were broken.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Being off normal duties for more than 7 days from a work accident is reportable under RIDDOR regardless of whether a bone was broken.

answers.text: You should personally phone the HSE before telling your manager.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: RIDDOR reports are made by the responsible person (your employer). Your role is to report internally promptly and accurately.

answers.text: Wait to see if the colleague complains before recording anything.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Incidents must be recorded promptly and factually as soon as they happen, not delayed.
